assessment: The Locations tab does its job at a basic CRUD level — you can add a hierarchical location and see existing ones — but the hierarchy is buried under a field matrix that reads like a flat form. Five equally-weighted inputs (Site, Zone, Rack, Shelf, Bin) suggest all fields matter equally, when in fact only Site is required and the rest are nested children. The existing locations list is a tiny card with cryptic `A > 1 > 1 > 1` path strings and no grouping by Site, which will collapse the moment a warehouse has more than a handful of bins. There is no search, no bulk import, and no way to add a child to an existing parent — every new bin forces you to retype the whole Site.
notable_strengths: Clear site/zone/rack/shelf/bin mental model is surfaced as column labels with `(optional)` hints on non-required fields.
Master Data sub-tabs (Product Metadata, Locations, Categories, Grades, Tags) keep reference-data tools co-located and predictable.
Edit and delete affordances on each existing location row are immediate and discoverable.

findings.title: Flat 5-column form hides the hierarchy and forces re-entry of parents
findings.what_is_wrong: The Add New Location card shows Site, Zone, Rack, Shelf, Bin as five equal-weight inputs side by side. Nothing in the UI signals that these are nested, and there is no way to pick an existing Site/Zone and append a child — every new bin requires retyping `WH1 > A > R1 > S1` from scratch.
findings.why_it_matters: Warehouses have dozens to hundreds of bins under the same site. Re-entering the parent chain on every add is the single biggest time cost on this page and also a consistency risk (typos create orphan siblings like `WH1` vs `wh1`).
findings.proposed_fix: Replace the flat row with a cascading picker: Site becomes a combobox of existing sites plus a 'Create new site' option; Zone/Rack/Shelf/Bin become dependent comboboxes that auto-fill from the selected parent. Add an 'Add child to…' action on each row in Existing Locations that pre-fills the parent chain.
findings.functionality_preserved: Preserves the Locations master-data entity (`SETTINGS_SECTIONS` Master Data › Locations) — all five fields remain writable, the `Add Location` submit still posts the full Site/Zone/Rack/Shelf/Bin tuple, and manual free-text creation stays available via the 'Create new' option.

findings.title: Existing Locations list is unreadable at scale — no grouping, search, or decoded path
findings.what_is_wrong: The Existing Locations section shows a single card 'Main Warehouse — A > 1 > 1 > 1' with no Site grouping, no search input, no pagination, no column headers naming what A/1/1/1 are, and no counter of total locations. With 50+ bins this becomes a scroll wall of indistinguishable breadcrumb strings.
findings.why_it_matters: Admins use this page to audit and fix warehouse geography before intake/shipping runs. If you can't find or scan a location quickly, duplicate bins get created and receiving routes to the wrong slot.
findings.proposed_fix: Group Existing Locations by Site with collapsible sections and a count ('Main Warehouse · 24 locations'). Add a search input above the list ('Search by site, zone, rack…'). Change each row to show labeled segments ('Zone A · Rack 1 · Shelf 1 · Bin 1') instead of a raw `>` string, and keep the pencil/trash icons on the right.
findings.functionality_preserved: Preserves the Locations listing/edit/delete behavior — the same records render, and the existing pencil (edit) and trash (delete) controls remain on every row; only grouping, labeling, and search are layered on top.

redesign_sketch: Keep the 'System Settings' H1 and Master Data › Locations tab chain. Replace the 'Add New Location' card with a compact two-line composer: line 1 is a Site combobox (existing sites + 'Create new'); line 2 reveals Zone → Rack → Shelf → Bin as dependent comboboxes that cascade from the selection, ending in a primary 'Add Location' button. Below, 'Existing Locations' becomes a searchable, grouped tree: a search box, then collapsible Site headers ('Main Warehouse · 24 bins') each containing rows labeled 'Zone A · Rack 1 · Shelf 1 · Bin 1' with a hover 'Add child' action plus the existing edit/delete icons. Footer hint: 'Tip: only Site is required — leave deeper levels blank for site-level storage.'
